assertion_results.text: The answer recommends using pytest fixtures for the shared setup
assertion_results.evidence: "What mechanism to use: Pytest fixtures (with yield for setup/teardown) and appropriate scopes."

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions `conftest.py` as the place for fixtures shared across multiple test modules
assertion_results.evidence: "Put the shared setup in a conftest.py located at the lowest common ancestor directory of the tests that need it (often tests/conftest.py). Pytest will auto-discover fixtures there for all tests under that directory."

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions choosing an appropriate fixture scope such as function, module, or session
assertion_results.evidence: Mentions "session- or module-scoped" for temp_db, "function- or module-scoped" for client, and "Choose scope based on isolation needs: session ... or function scope for full isolation."
